title: Radioactive Decay Simulator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive MicroSim visualizing exponential decay of radioactive atoms with adjustable half-life and real-time decay curve plotting.

learningObjectives: Visualize exponential decay and predict remaining radioactivity after multiple half-lives
Explain how individual probabilistic events produce predictable population-level patterns
Calculate the fraction of radioactive material remaining after a given number of half-lives
Connect radioactive decay to environmental issues including nuclear waste management
prerequisites: Basic concept of atoms and isotopes

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Visual simulation connecting probabilistic micro-events to macro-patterns
keyQuestions: After 4 half-lives, what fraction of the original material remains?
Why does the decay curve never reach exactly zero?
How long must nuclear waste be stored to be considered safe?
How is radiometric dating used to study ecological history?
commonMisconceptions: All radioactive material decays at the same rate
After two half-lives, all the radioactive material is gone
Individual atoms decay at predictable times rather than probabilistically
assessmentOpportunities: Students predict remaining atoms after N half-lives and verify with the simulation
Students calculate storage time for nuclear waste using half-life data
Students compare observed decay curves to the mathematical formula
